role_title: Strategic Business Performance, Central Strategy & Operations

seniority.level: Staff / Principal IC or Senior Director-equivalent
level_signals: Leads high-stakes interventions with senior stakeholders

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 12+ years of experience in product/data analytics within product organizations or strategic business performance teams at high-growth tech companies.
Experience in Product Strategy, Product Management, or GM-style roles where you owned a P&L or a specific business outcome.
Deeply curious about business models and data, probing into signals, understanding trends and customer usage patterns to uncover novel insights
A proven track record of directly driving business growth through analytical insights and execution.
Apply AI to enhance decision-making and operational efficiency, know when and how to use the right AI tools to move faster without sacrificing rigor or quality.
Strong product sense and the ability to understand the technical mechanics of how software products are built and scaled.
Experience navigating ambiguous, cross-functional problems and leading high-stakes interventions with senior stakeholders.
Experience facilitating senior leadership/ stakeholder meetings to drive meaningful conversations regarding Stripe's business performance.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Prior experience with fintech products or complex financial systems is a plus, but not required.
Payments ecosystem, or experience in finance or fintech is a plus
Ability to build trust and partner with senior leaders and CXOs
Technical proficiency in SQL  / Python / R and data analysis; ability to self-serve and perform independent deep-dives into complex datasets.
Experience driving creative solutions with and effectively informing the roadmap of cross-functional teams
Ability to understand the implications of Stripe ships such as product features, policy changes, and marketing outreach on the user experience and resulting support experience

scope.surface: Central Strategy & Operations — Strategic Business Performance (SBP): cross-company performance signal, executive reporting, surge interventions, product-strategy bridging
core_responsibilities: Define and maintain the metrics that matter for in-year performance; develop a 'morning read' discipline
Lead 'surge' projects to diagnose and unblock critical goals at risk
Translate long-horizon corporate theses into in-year operational reality across product, risk, and marketing
Prepare CEO/COO-level materials that translate unstructured signals into structured diagnoses
Work with Corporate Strategy and Product teams to pressure-test assumptions against external market forces

green_flags: Role explicitly described as 'small, high-leverage group' — suggests significant individual impact
Direct exposure to CEO/COO and executive team
Fintech/payments experience listed as preferred, not required — opens field of candidates
Encourages application from those meeting minimum requirements even without preferred qualifications
red_flags: No location, remote policy, or work authorization information stated anywhere in the JD
12+ years minimum is a high bar that may filter out strong mid-career candidates
Role title is ambiguous — 'Strategic Business Performance, Central Strategy & Operations' could encompass many archetypes, making positioning difficult

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 12+ years requirement, CEO/COO-level scope, and 'senior product strategist-operator' descriptor)
scope.business_model (inferred from Stripe's public product description)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
ambiguities: The JD lists 'Prior experience with fintech products or complex financial systems is a plus, but not required.' under Minimum Requirements, yet the same theme appears again under Preferred Qualifications as 'Payments ecosystem, or experience in finance or fintech is a plus'. Both have been captured separately — the first as a must_have with soft severity (explicitly flagged as not required), the second as a nice_to_have.

The role title and description suggest a BizOps/Strategy & Ops archetype, but the responsibilities and requirements blend heavily with Technical PM and Product Strategy; archetype assigned as BizOps/Strategy & Ops with secondary Technical PM.
manages_people is inferred as false — the JD mentions leading cross-functional interventions but does not describe direct reports.
